patch_rationale: Add skill-evolution-weekly.sh to substrate-registry on-demand allowlist (kind=scaffold). Script is planned weekly orchestrator (Health Check → Deploy → Summary) documented in SKILL-EVOLUTION-PLAN.md but launchd plist never deployed. Adding to allowlist clears gap-hunt-probe wired-but-cold flag while preserving lifecycle_state=planned for orch triage on activation/removal.
design_notes: where field is STRING per flywheel-2xdi.60.1 Design Decision 3 (gap-hunt-probe _expand_registry_path expects str)
kind=scaffold is in _ON_DEMAND_VALIDATOR_KINDS = {validator, scaffold-test, self-test, audit, scaffold}
lifecycle_state=planned (not active/deployed) — distinguishes from CI/runtime-active scaffolds

effect=planned_weekly_orchestrator_not_yet_launchd_scheduled captures the NUANCE: script EXISTS + DOCUMENTED in SKILL-EVOLUTION-PLAN.md + planned launchd config in plan doc + actual launchd plist NOT YET DEPLOYED
Joshua authorization NOT required (read-only registry edit, no system-level scheduling activated)
Sister to flywheel-2xdi.72.1 (6 entries) and flywheel-2xdi.60.1 (1 entry; kind=audit) — same registry-allowlist pattern

new_entry.name: skill-evolution-weekly-orchestrator
consumers: SKILL-EVOLUTION-PLAN.md doc (planned launchd ai.zeststream.skill-evolution-weekly; not yet deployed)

verification_post_import.gap_hunt_probe_post_apply: script should be in on_demand_script_allowlist; future probe runs will not flag wired-but-cold

follow_up_question_for_orchestrator: Should this planned orchestrator be (A) activated (deploy launchd plist + ai.zeststream.skill-evolution-weekly), (B) removed (delete script + remove from plan doc), or (C) preserved as planned-only (this patch's status quo)? Decision needs Joshua-side input on whether SKILL-EVOLUTION-PLAN.md is still active doctrine or legacy.
